subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Article IV, World Bank, WJP, RSF, V-Dem) + Thai official (Bank of Thailand, NESDC) + independent/regional press (Bangkok Post, Nikkei Asia).

Media constraint: Thai domestic outlets operate under the lese-majeste (Article 112) and defamation laws; governance/politics/press were scored on observable ACTIONS (court rulings, sentences, prosecution counts) cross-checked against international rights monitors, not domestic framing.
Standout drags: chronic political/judicial instability, restricted civil liberties, an active border conflict, and a hard demographic/belonging ceiling.

economic.notes: A credible central bank and a diversified export/tourism base, against slow growth, deflation risk, narrowing fiscal space, and very high household debt.

fiscal_state.notes: Public debt ~65% GDP and approaching the ceiling; space narrowing; 2025 stimulus targeted; the IMF urges consolidation. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: The Bank of Thailand runs a credible managed float; recent easing is data-driven but coincides with government pressure to cut - genuine independence with friction. (BoT; IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The baht is an own currency, not a reserve currency. (IMF)

inflation.notes: Headline near zero/slightly negative (~-0.1% in 2025), below target; a deflationary risk slightly caps the positive. (BoT; IMF)

banking_stability.notes: Well-capitalised and judged resilient, but household debt ~90% GDP and an impaired credit channel are material risks. (IMF 2025 Art IV; BoT)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A diversified auto/electronics export base plus tourism, no resource curse; the US reciprocal tariff settled at 19% (off a threatened 36%); a strong baht and over-reliance on exports the offsets. (USTR; BoT)

institutional.notes: Usable economic data and a competent central bank, against a politicized judiciary and a constrained press.

rule_of_law.notes: WJP 77/143, below the regional average; the judiciary is repeatedly used to remove elected PMs and dissolve parties - politicized on political matters, though commercial law functions. (WJP 2025; HRW)

statistical_integrity.notes: Economic data is IMF-validated and usable. (IMF 2025 Art IV; BoT)

civil_service_capacity.notes: A competent central bank/technocracy with uneven delivery and rural gaps. (IMF; WJP)

press_freedom.notes: RSF 85/180 (best in ASEAN) but the lese-majeste law (15-year sentences) hangs over every outlet, forcing self-censorship. (RSF 2025; OHCHR)

political_social.notes: Chronic instability and restricted civil liberties; easy lawful entry but a hard belonging ceiling and a serious demographic drag.

political_stability.notes: The PM was removed by the Constitutional Court in Aug-2025 (the fifth PM ousted by judges since 2008); monarchy/military influence persists - clearly volatile. (Time; Al Jazeera 2025)

civil_liberties.notes: 285+ lese-majeste prosecutions since 2014, with multi-decade sentences and free-speech imprisonments into 2026. (HRW 2026; OHCHR)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: LTR/Elite/retirement visas give easy lawful entry on income/wealth, but with no path to citizenship/belonging and work restrictions - a hard ceiling. (Thai visa rules)

social_cohesion.notes: A persistent southern (Malay-Muslim) insurgency plus deep political/regional polarization. (HRW)

demographics.notes: Ageing fast with fertility ~1.0-1.2 - 'old before rich,' a real long-term drag. (UN; IMF 2025)

geopolitical.notes: A reliable-but-hedging US ally with an active border conflict.

alliance_reliability.notes: A US treaty ally that hedges China heavily - reliable but non-aligned. (observed)

conflict_involvement.notes: A 2025 Cambodia border war (100+ dead, ~500k displaced) with a fragile Dec-2025 ceasefire, Myanmar-border spillover, and the southern insurgency. (NPR/CNN Dec-2025)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: A relatively open capital account; the BoT tightened baht monitoring in 2025 but with no hard controls. (BoT; IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Standout affordable universal healthcare and decent core infrastructure, against serious flood/heat exposure.

climate.notes: Bangkok delta subsidence/sea-level, flooding (2011), drought, coastal exposure, moderate readiness. (WB CCDR; IPCC AR6)

healthcare.notes: Strong, affordable, with universal coverage and a medical-tourism hub. (IMF; observed)

infrastructure.notes: Bangkok strong (transit, airports, telecom); rural areas uneven. (observed)

crime_safety.notes: Moderate; generally safe for residents and visitors. (observed)

disaster_insurance.notes: Material flood exposure; the market is developing post-2011. (observed)

summary: Easy lawful entry, excellent affordable universal healthcare, and a credible central bank, set against chronic political and judicial instability (PMs repeatedly removed by the courts), restricted civil liberties under the lese-majeste law, an active border conflict, and a hard demographic and belonging ceiling. Serious flood/heat exposure adds physical risk.
